documentation.file_docstring: Inbound webhook handler for Synology Chat outgoing webhooks. Parses form-urlencoded/JSON body, validates security, delivers to agent.
inline_comments: One rate limiter per account, created lazily

Parse/normalize incoming webhook payload. Supports: - application/x-www-form-urlencoded - application/json Token resolution order: body.token -> query.token -> headers Field aliases: - user_id <- user_id | userId | user - text    <- text | message | content
Fallback for clients with missing/incorrect content-type.
Try JSON first, then form-urlencoded.

Create an HTTP request handler for Synology Chat outgoing webhooks. This handler: 1. Parses form-urlencoded/JSON payload 2. Validates token (constant-time) 3. Checks user allowlist 4. Checks rate limit 5. Sanitizes input 6. Immediately ACKs request (204) 7. Delivers to the agent asynchronously and sends final reply via incomingUrl
Only accept POST
Parse body
Parse payload
Token validation
DM policy authorization
Rate limit
Sanitize input
Strip trigger word
ACK immediately so Synology Chat won't remain in "Processing..."
Default to webhook user_id; may be replaced with Chat API user_id below.
Deliver to agent asynchronously (with 120s timeout to match nginx proxy_read_timeout)
Resolve the Chat-internal user_id for sending replies.
Synology Chat outgoing webhooks use a per-integration user_id that may
differ from the global Chat API user_id required by method=chatbot.
We resolve via the user_list API, matching by nickname/username.
Send reply back to Synology Chat using the resolved Chat user_id
